definition: Private credit ($1.7-2.0T) has structurally altered how credit risk manifests by deferring impairment recognition by 6-18 months. BDC and interval fund NAVs marked quarterly using manager-selected comparables show an estimated 15-25% gap between reported NAVs and probable recovery values for bottom-quartile portfolios. Semi-liquid vehicle risk (quarterly/monthly redemption at NAV against illiquid assets) creates a classic liquidity spiral setup.
evidence: Quarterly NAV marking using manager-selected comparables
Estimated 15-25% NAV gap for bottom-quartile portfolios
Combined with non-GAAP and EBITDA addbacks, estimated 40-50% of monetary transmission invisible to conventional metrics
open_questions: Is private credit deferring or structurally changing credit cycle dynamics (Japanese forbearance analogy)?
When will semi-liquid vehicle redemption pressure materialize?
What is the true aggregate NAV gap across the private credit market?
Does the 40-50% invisible estimate survive scrutiny (estimate built on estimates)?
